---
title: "ShinyApps Web Traffic Monitoring"
format: 
  dashboard:
    theme: litera
    orientation: rows
    embed-resources: true
---

<!-- 
HOW TO RUN THIS DASHBOARD FOR CODE REVIEW:
Option 1: In Positron or RStudio, open this file and click the "Render" button at the top.
Option 2: Run the following in the R Console: `quarto::quarto_render("Report/dashboard.qmd")`
Option 3: Run the following in the Terminal: `quarto preview Report/dashboard.qmd`
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)
library(plotly)
library(safepaths)
library(glue)
library(DT)

# Define paths based on your 00-setup.R logic
# and the working directory during render is the R/ folder.
LAN_FOLDER <- use_network_path()
PROJECT_ROOT <- glue("{LAN_FOLDER}/0. Misc/Data Science Tooling/web-hosting-and-dashboards/shinyapps_webtraffic_monitoring")
OUTPUT_TABLES <- glue("{PROJECT_ROOT}/outputs/tables")

# Load summary data for value boxes
weekly_usage <- read_csv(file.path(OUTPUT_TABLES, "weekly_usage.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
visits_summary <- read_csv(file.path(OUTPUT_TABLES, "visits_minmax_summary.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
concurrency_summary <- read_csv(file.path(OUTPUT_TABLES, "concurrency_summary.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

app_abbr_map <- c(
  "Student Outcomes" = "SO",
  "popApp" = "POP",
  "LAEP" = "LAEP",
  "BCDS-DIP Linkage Rates" = "DIP",
  "BC Small Business" = "SB",
  "Economic-Indicators" = "ECON",
  "Household Projections" = "HHLD",
  "LFS app" = "LFS",
  "Interprovincial Migration" = "IM",
  "Country Trade Profiles" = "TRADE",
  "BC Retail Sales" = "RS"
)

weekly_usage <- weekly_usage |>
  mutate(app_abbr = coalesce(unname(app_abbr_map[pageTitle]), pageTitle))

concurrency_summary <- concurrency_summary |>
  mutate(app_abbr = coalesce(unname(app_abbr_map[app_name]), app_name))

earliest_week <- min(weekly_usage$week, na.rm = TRUE)
latest_week <- max(weekly_usage$week, na.rm = TRUE)
date_range_str <- paste(format(earliest_week, "%b %d, %Y"), "-", format(latest_week, "%b %d, %Y"))
latest_week_data <- weekly_usage |> filter(week == latest_week)

top_app <- latest_week_data |> arrange(desc(total_sessions)) |> slice(1) |> pull(pageTitle)
latest_visits <- sum(latest_week_data$total_sessions, na.rm = TRUE)
peak_concurrent <- max(concurrency_summary$max_concurrent, na.rm = TRUE)

previous_week <- max(weekly_usage$week[weekly_usage$week < latest_week], na.rm = TRUE)
previous_week_data <- weekly_usage |> filter(week == previous_week)
prev_visits <- sum(previous_week_data$total_sessions, na.rm = TRUE)

pct_change <- if (prev_visits > 0) {
  round((latest_visits - prev_visits) / prev_visits * 100, 1)
} else {
  NA
}
abs_change <- latest_visits - prev_visits

pct_change_str <- if (!is.na(pct_change)) {
  abs_sign <- if (abs_change > 0) "+" else ""
  paste0(abs_sign, abs_change, " (", abs_sign, pct_change, "%) vs prev week")
} else {
  ""
}
```

# Executive Summary

<p style="text-align: center; font-size: 1.1em; color: #555; margin-bottom: 2px;"><i>Data from: `r date_range_str`</i></p>

## Row {height=45%}

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Most Popular App (Latest Week)"
list(
  icon = "star",
  color = "primary",
  value = top_app
)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
title_str <- paste0("Total Visits (Latest Week", if(pct_change_str != "") paste0(" | ", pct_change_str) else "", ")")
list(
  icon = "graph-up",
  color = "info",
  value = latest_visits,
  title = title_str
)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Max Concurrent Users"
list(
  icon = "people-fill",
  color = "success",
  value = peak_concurrent
)
```

## Row {height=55%}

### Weekly App Visits Trend

```{r}
visits_trend_plot <- weekly_usage |>
  filter(week >= as.Date("2025-07-01")) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = week, y = total_sessions, color = pageTitle, group = pageTitle)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Weekly App Visits Trend<br><sup style='font-size:14px; font-weight:normal; color:#555;'><i>Tip: Click an app in the legend to isolate its trend line or reset.</i></sup>",
    x = "Week (from July 2025)",
    y = "Total Visits (Sessions)",
    color = "App Name"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 18),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14)
  )

# Convert to plotly with default interactive legend toggles
ggplotly(visits_trend_plot, tooltip = c("x", "y", "group")) |>
  layout(legend = list(itemclick = "toggleothers", itemdoubleclick = "toggle"))
```

### Maximum Concurrent Visits

```{r}
max_concurrent_plot <- concurrency_summary |>
  filter(interval == "1m") |> # Use 1-minute buckets for true peak
  mutate(app_abbr = fct_reorder(app_abbr, max_concurrent, .desc = TRUE)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = app_abbr, y = max_concurrent, fill = app_name)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = max_concurrent), vjust = -0.5, size = 5) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) + # Add space for labels
  labs(
    title = "Maximum Concurrent Visits by App",
    subtitle = "Based on 1-minute interval peaks",
    x = "App",
    y = "Max Concurrent Users",
    fill = "App Name"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14)
  )

ggplotly(max_concurrent_plot, tooltip = c("fill", "y"))
```


# App Details {scrolling="true"}

<p style="text-align: center; font-size: 1.1em; color: #555; margin-bottom: 2px;"><i>Data from: `r date_range_str`</i></p>

## Row {.tabset height=800px}

### Traffic Distribution (High Traffic)

```{r}
#| expandable: false
high_traffic_apps <- c("popApp", "Student Outcomes")

visits_minmax_plot_high <- weekly_usage |>
  filter(pageTitle %in% high_traffic_apps) |>
  mutate(
    app_abbr = fct_reorder(app_abbr, total_sessions, .fun = max, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = app_abbr, y = total_sessions, fill = pageTitle)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    alpha = 0.7,
    outlier.size = 3
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Weekly Visits (High Traffic Apps)",
    subtitle = "Box shows 25th to 75th percentile, line is median, dots are outliers",
    x = "App",
    y = "Weekly Visits",
    fill = "App Name"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14)
  )

ggplotly(visits_minmax_plot_high)
```

### Traffic Distribution (Low Traffic)

```{r}
#| expandable: false
visits_minmax_plot_low <- weekly_usage |>
  filter(!pageTitle %in% high_traffic_apps) |>
  mutate(
    app_abbr = fct_reorder(app_abbr, total_sessions, .fun = max, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = app_abbr, y = total_sessions, fill = pageTitle)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    alpha = 0.7,
    outlier.size = 3
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(
    ylim = c(
      0,
      quantile(
        weekly_usage |>
          filter(!pageTitle %in% high_traffic_apps) |>
          pull(total_sessions),
        0.95,
        na.rm = TRUE
      )
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Weekly Visits (Low Traffic Apps)",
    subtitle = "Box shows 25th to 75th percentile, line is median, dots are outliers (Y-axis zoomed)",
    x = "App",
    y = "Weekly Visits",
    fill = "App Name"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14)
  )

ggplotly(visits_minmax_plot_low)
```

### Trend by App (High Traffic)

```{r}
#| expandable: false
visits_trend_faceted_high <- weekly_usage |>
  filter(week >= as.Date("2025-07-01"), pageTitle %in% high_traffic_apps) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = week, y = total_sessions, color = app_abbr)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~app_abbr) +
  labs(
    title = "Weekly App Visits Trend (High Traffic)",
    x = "Week (from July 2025)",
    y = "Total Visits"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(3, "lines") 
  )

ggplotly(visits_trend_faceted_high)
```

### Trend by App (Low Traffic)

```{r}
#| expandable: false
visits_trend_faceted_low <- weekly_usage |>
  filter(week >= as.Date("2025-07-01"), !pageTitle %in% high_traffic_apps) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = week, y = total_sessions, color = app_abbr)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~app_abbr) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 140)) +
  labs(
    title = "Weekly App Visits Trend (Low Traffic)",
    x = "Week (from July 2025)",
    y = "Total Visits"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(3, "lines") 
  )

ggplotly(visits_trend_faceted_low)
```

## Row

### Summary Statistics Table

```{r}
visits_summary_clean <- visits_summary |>
  mutate(
    app_abbr = coalesce(unname(app_abbr_map[pageTitle]), pageTitle),
    pageTitle = paste0(pageTitle, " (", app_abbr, ")")
  ) |>
  select(-app_abbr) |>
  rename(
    `App Name` = pageTitle,
    `Min Weekly Visits` = min_weekly_visits,
    `Median Weekly Visits` = median_weekly_visits,
    `Avg Weekly Visits` = avg_weekly_visits,
    `Max Weekly Visits` = max_weekly_visits
  ) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), round, 1))

datatable(visits_summary_clean, options = list(pageLength = 11, dom = 't'))
```
